title: Dynamic Programming States, Transitions, and Reconstruction · Structure Brief: Dp Correctness
summary: Optimize resource allocation where overlapping choices defeat local heuristics. Planned artifact: Add and verify the dsa2 dp correctness increment in a bottom-up optimizer with reconstructed decisions. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Prove state meaning and transition completeness by induction.
Store or recompute choices to recover an optimal solution, not only its value.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
